---
title: "Account assistant profile"
description: "ReadOnlyAccess plus targeted writes — for agents that explain, audit, and lightly tag."
---

<Info>
Account assistant gives the agent `ReadOnlyAccess` (managed policy) plus a small allow-list of writes for tagging and minor housekeeping. Use it when you want an agent that *understands* your account but shouldn't be creating/deleting infra.
</Info>

## What it is

A middle ground between full builder and zero-AWS personal assistant. The agent can:

- Describe, list, and get any AWS resource
- Read CloudWatch metrics, logs, Cost Explorer
- Tag resources (housekeeping)
- Write to its own CloudWatch Logs group

It **cannot**:

- Create, modify, or delete AWS resources
- Assume other roles
- Modify IAM

## Defaults (simple mode)

| Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| IAM policy | `arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnlyAccess` + targeted tag/log writes |
| Instance type | `t4g.medium` (2 vCPU, 4 GiB, arm64) |
| Root volume | 40 GB |
| Data volume | 80 GB (OpenClaw) / 0 GB (stateless packs) |
| Security Hub | ✅ enabled |
| GuardDuty | ✅ enabled |
| Inspector | ✅ enabled |
| Access Analyzer | ✅ enabled |
| Config recorder | ✅ enabled |

## Install examples

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="OpenClaw account assistant">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack openclaw \
      --profile account_assistant
    ```
    OpenClaw gateway that can explain any resource in the account. Good for ops / SRE workflows.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Claude Code account assistant">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack claude-code \
      --profile account_assistant
    ```
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## When to use account assistant

- **Audit / explain agents** — "summarize this VPC", "what's costing us", "why is this Lambda slow"
- **Dev or staging accounts** where the agent shouldn't ship code but should help debug
- **Sensitive accounts** where builder is too much but you still want the agent to see everything

## Upgrading later

If the agent later needs write access, redeploy with `--profile builder`. Profiles are baked into the instance profile — they can't be flipped in place.
